 {"id":"bd-1idx","title":"TEST: Rate limit declarations integration (host/CLI)","description":"Integration coverage for rate limit declarations once host/CLI surfaces exist.

Goal:
- Verify host aggregates RateLimitDeclarations from multiple connectors.
- Verify CLI introspection exposes per-tool rate limits in JSON.

Notes:
- Currently no host/CLI aggregation surfaces found; unblock when fcp-host or CLI introspect is implemented.

Acceptance:
- Integration test harness installs 2+ connectors with declared pools and asserts aggregated limits.
- CLI command returns rate limit metadata in tool descriptors.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBear","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:35:04.362270678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:18:45.635192850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1im5","title":"[IDEA-20.2] Unit tests for audit chain verification","description":"## Coverage
- Valid chain passes.
- Missing link fails with expected code.
- Fork detection triggers failure.

## Logging
- Structured logs include head seq and fork location.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberElk","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:43:40.273899002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:39:21.051608384Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:39:21.051528606Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1im5","depends_on_id":"bd-1did","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:43:45.618543746Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1im5","depends_on_id":"bd-2gp9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:43:40.283771683Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jdh","title":"[IDEA-24] Connector Packaging + Reproducible Build Tooling","description":"## Background
Supply chain integrity requires reproducible builds, manifest embedding, and SBOM generation. These steps are currently manual or implicit.

## Goals
- Provide a packaging tool that embeds manifest, signs binary, and emits SBOM.
- Verify reproducibility (hash match) in CI.
- Produce artifacts compatible with registry verification.

## Non-goals
- Replacing registry verification logic (separate concern).

## Approach
- Add `fcp package` or `fcp build-connector` tool.
- Generate SBOM (CycloneDX or SPDX) and build attestations.
- Store build metadata in a deterministic envelope object.

## Risks
- Platform-specific build variance; require deterministic build flags.

## Success Criteria
- Build output is reproducible across runs on the same platform.
- Packaged artifacts pass verification checks.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Two successive builds produce identical hashes.
- SBOM and manifest signature are included in package output.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:46:13.581271243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:47:50.035282466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:47:50.035208779Z","close_reason":"All subtasks completed:
- bd-1oz9: Implemented connector packaging CLI (fcp package)
- bd-232x: Unit tests for packaging metadata
- bd-6phb: E2E reproducible build check - builds same connector twice and verifies identical SHA-256 hashes

Acceptance criteria met:
- Two successive builds produce identical hashes (verified in reproducible_build_produces_identical_hash test)
- Build metadata, manifest, and SBOM generation implemented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1nq6","title":"[IDEA-15] fcp doctor: Connector Self-Check Plugins","description":"# fcp doctor: Connector Self-Check Plugins

## Goal
Allow connectors to register lightweight self-checks (auth validity, API reachability, quota status) that `fcp doctor` can invoke safely and uniformly.

## Background / Why
Operators need a standardized way to verify connector readiness without invoking real operations. A plugin mechanism enables reusable health checks per connector.

## Scope
- SDK trait for self-checks (read-only, bounded, no side effects).
- Host/CLI integration in `fcp doctor`.

## Deliverables
- `SelfCheck` trait and registration mechanism.
- `fcp doctor --connector <id>` calls the self-check.
- Structured output with reason codes.

## Tests
- Unit tests for self-check dispatch and error mapping.
- Integration test with a mock connector self-check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fcp doctor` can report connector-specific readiness without side effects.
- Output is consistent and JSON schema-stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:44:10.996931069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:47:26.830728016Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nq6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T19:47:26.830681600Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz9","title":"[IDEA-24.1] Implement connector packaging CLI","description":"## What
Implement packaging workflow: build, embed manifest, sign, and emit SBOM.

## How
- Invoke cargo build with deterministic flags.
- Embed manifest section and signature metadata.
- Output SBOM + build metadata JSON.

## Success Criteria
- CLI emits a single package directory with binary + manifest + SBOM.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Packaging fails if manifest embedding is invalid.
- Output directory layout is stable and documented.
